title: Wet-felted laptop sleeve
subtitle: A form-fitting merino sleeve for a 13-inch laptop, wet-felted over a stiff foam resist and fulled to 8 mm thickness
excerpt: A close-fitting merino laptop sleeve, wet-felted over a foam resist cut to match a 13-inch laptop. Eight millimetres of dense fulled felt provides scratch and impact protection. The resist is removed mid-process and the piece is fulled to exact dimensions before drying on the laptop itself.
sourceNotes: Wet-felted laptop sleeves are a precision resist project common in UK and European felting classes. Method synthesised from flat-resist wet-felting practice.

glossaryTerms.definition: A firm form placed inside roving layers during wet felting to create an opening or hollow in the finished piece.
Combed and drawn wool fibres in a continuous rope, the raw material for wet felting.
Vigorous agitation applied after initial felting to compress and shrink the fabric to its intended density and size.
The reduction in size that occurs during felting and fulling. A typical merino felt piece shrinks 25-35% in each dimension from its pre-felt size.

content.text: A laptop sleeve requires more precision than most felting projects: the opening must fit the device closely, and the walls must be thick enough to protect the screen and body. The
resist
must be cut oversize to account for the shrinkage (approximately 30%) that occurs during
fulling
. The instructions below are sized for a 13-inch laptop (approximately 32 × 22 × 1.5 cm). Scale the resist dimensions proportionally for other sizes.

attrs.title: Sizing note
attrs.body: Measure the actual laptop before cutting the resist. Add 30% to each dimension for the resist size, then add 2 cm on all sides for the edge fold. The finished sleeve should be 2-3 mm larger than the laptop when dry.

items.name: Merino roving, contrasting colour (optional decorative layer)
Foam pool noodle or upholstery foam sheet, cut to 45 × 30 × 2 cm (resist)
Plastic sheet or bin bag (to cover the resist)

content.text: Method
Step 1: Cut and prepare the resist
The resist for a 13-inch laptop (32 × 22 cm body) should be cut to 45 × 30 cm. This accounts for 30%
take-up
during fulling. Cover the resist in a plastic sheet or bag so the roving does not bond to it.
Step 2: Lay roving on both sides
Place the resist flat on the felting mat. Lay four cross-hatch layers of
roving

on the first side, extending 3 cm beyond all edges. Fold the overhanging roving tightly over the edges and onto the underside. Flip the resist and lay four more cross-hatch layers on the second side, folding the overhang over the remaining edges. Press the edge folds firmly against the surface. The total thickness of roving on each face should be approximately 2 cm before wetting.
Step 3: Wet thoroughly and begin rubbing

Mix soapy water (45°C) and saturate both sides of the roving-covered resist completely. Place the felting net over the first side and rub in small circular movements for 10 minutes per side, beginning with light pressure and building to firm. Flip and repeat on the other side. The piece should feel stable and the fibres should not pull away when pinched.
Step 4: Remove the resist and roll

Carefully slide the foam resist out through the short open end of the sleeve. Roll the sleeve in the bamboo mat and roll back and forth for 5 minutes. Unroll, rotate 90 degrees, and roll again. Repeat three more times, re-wetting with hot soapy water as needed. During rolling, check the walls with a thumb and forefinger: they should be at least 8 mm thick when compressed.
Step 5: Full and check dimensions

Throw the sleeve against the felting mat twenty times, rotating between throws. Check the dimensions every 5 minutes: the sleeve should measure approximately 34 × 24 cm at this stage (it will continue to shrink slightly during drying). When the felt is dense and the opening dimensions are within 1 cm of the laptop's dimensions, stop fulling.
Step 6: Dry on the laptop

Rinse the sleeve in cool water. Squeeze out excess water. Slip the laptop (inside a plastic bag to protect it from moisture) into the sleeve and leave to dry completely, approximately 24 hours. The felt will dry to the exact shape of the laptop, giving a snug fit.

items.problem: The sleeve is too small to slide the laptop in after drying
items.cause: Over-fulling reduced the dimensions below the laptop's size
items.fix: Re-wet the dry sleeve, stretch it gently over a book or foam block 1-2 cm wider than the laptop and leave to dry in the stretched position.

items.problem: The sleeve walls are thinner than 8 mm in places
items.cause: Roving layers were uneven during laying, or the edge folds were not pressed firmly enough
items.fix: Before the piece is fully dry, add patches of roving to the thin areas and needle-felt them in with a size 38 needle to blend the edges. Then re-wet and roll briefly to integrate the added layer.

items.problem: The two faces of the sleeve bonded across the opening
items.cause: The roving was folded too far over the resist edges and the layers from both sides joined at the opening
items.fix: Slip scissors inside the opening and cut the bond. The dense felt will not fray. Work the opening open carefully.

content.text: Care
Hand-wash in cool water with wool-safe detergent only when necessary. Dry over the laptop or a same-size book. The sleeve can be re-shaped slightly when damp if it begins to lose its fit over time.
